steps.title: Find the key evidence in the passage
steps.content: The passage describes Elinor as her mother's "counsellor" despite being "though only nineteen." She possesses "coolness of judgment," gives "effectual" advice, and "knew how to govern" her feelings — "a knowledge which her mother had yet to learn." The emphasis on her youth paired with her advanced judgment is the decisive signal.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Elinor often argues with her mother but fails to change her mind.: The passage says her advice was "effectual," meaning it worked. She does not fail. ✗
  • Choice B — Elinor can be overly sensitive with regard to family matters.: The passage says she has "coolness of judgment" and governs her feelings — the opposite of oversensitivity. ✗
  • Choice C — Elinor thinks her mother is a bad role model.: The passage shows Elinor managing her mother's imprudence, but nowhere states Elinor's opinion of her mother as a role model. ✗
  • Choice D — Elinor is remarkably mature for her age.: Being nineteen yet serving as her mother's counselor with sound judgment and emotional control that her mother lacks establishes extraordinary maturity. ✓
D is correct.
